questions.answer: The gametophyte is the haploid phase of the life cycle of a plant, which produces gametes.

questions.q: What is the function of stigma?
questions.answer: The stigma is the uppermost part of the pistil that functions as the site of pollen reception.
key_points: Stigma is the site of pollen reception

questions.q: What is the process of fusion of male and female gametes?
questions.answer: The process of fusion of male and female gametes is called fertilization.
key_points: Fertilization is the fusion of gametes

questions.q: What is the function of nucellus?
questions.answer: The nucellus is a part of the ovule that provides nourishment to the developing embryo.

questions.q: What is the process of development of fruit?
questions.answer: The process of development of fruit is called fruit formation.
key_points: Fruit formation is a result of fertilization

questions.answer: The ovary is the lowermost part of the pistil that contains ovules. It is composed of one or more carpels and is usually located in the center of the flower.
key_points: Ovary is the lowermost part of pistil

questions.answer: Pollination is the transfer of pollen from the anther of one flower to the stigma of another flower. This can occur through various methods such as self-pollination, cross-pollination, and wind pollination.
key_points: Self-pollination and cross-pollination are two types of pollination
Wind pollination is another method of pollination

questions.answer: The anther is the pollen-producing part of the stamen. It is composed of four microsporocytes, each of which produces four microspores, which develop into pollen grains.
key_points: Anther is the pollen-producing part of stamen

questions.answer: Fertilization is the fusion of male and female gametes, resulting in the formation of a zygote. This process involves the germination of the pollen grain on the stigma, growth of the pollen tube through the style, and release of the sperm cells into the ovary.

questions.q: Describe the structure of pollen grain.
questions.answer: The pollen grain is a small, oval-shaped structure that contains two nuclei, a vegetative nucleus and a generative nucleus. It is composed of a sporopollenin wall and a cytoplasmic content.
key_points: Pollen grain is small and oval-shaped
Two nuclei are present in pollen grain
Sporopollenin is the wall of pollen grain

questions.answer: Fertilization is necessary for the development of a zygote, which eventually gives rise to a seedling. The fusion of male and female gametes results in the formation of a diploid cell, which is essential for the development of a new individual. Fertilization also ensures the genetic diversity of the offspring.
key_points: Fertilization is necessary for zygote formation

questions.q: Describe the process of fruit formation.
questions.answer: Fruit formation is the development of a fruit from the ovary of a flower. This process involves the growth and differentiation of the ovary wall, formation of seeds, and development of a pericarp. The type of fruit formed depends on the number of carpels present in the ovary.
key_points: Fruit formation involves growth of ovary wall

questions.q: Explain the process of seed formation.
questions.answer: Seed formation is the development of a seed from the ovule of a flower. This process involves the growth and differentiation of the ovule wall, formation of an embryo and endosperm, and development of a seed coat. The type of seed formed depends on the type of ovule present in the ovary.
key_points: Seed formation involves growth of ovule wall
Embryo and endosperm are formed inside seed

questions.answer: The ovule is a part of the ovary that contains a female gametophyte and an egg cell. It is composed of a nucellus, an integument, and an embryo sac. The female gametophyte is responsible for producing an egg cell, while the embryo sac provides nourishment to the developing embryo.
key_points: Ovule contains female gametophyte and egg cell
Nucellus and integument are present in ovule

questions.q: Explain the process of pollination and fertilization in flowering plants.
questions.answer: Pollination is the transfer of pollen from the anther of one flower to the stigma of another flower. Fertilization is the fusion of male and female gametes, resulting in the formation of a zygote. The process of pollination and fertilization involves the germination of the pollen grain on the stigma, growth of the pollen tube through the style, and release of the sperm cells into the ovary.
